problems.title: Two Sum
problems.description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target. You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.

problems.title: Valid Parentheses
problems.description: Given a string s containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid. Open brackets must be closed by the same type of brackets in the correct order.
learning_objectives: Handle edge cases for unmatched brackets

problems.title: Container With Most Water
problems.description: You are given an integer array height of length n. Find two lines that together with the x-axis form a container, such that the container contains the most water.
learning_objectives: Optimize brute force O(N^2) to O(N)

problems.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
problems.description: Given a string s, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
learning_objectives: Use sets for O(1) existence checks

problems.title: Group Anagrams
problems.description: Given an array of strings strs, group the anagrams together. You can return the answer in any order.

problems.title: Top K Frequent Elements
problems.description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the k most frequent elements. You may return the answer in any order.
problems.inefficient_solution:
def top_k_frequent(nums, k):
    from collections import Counter
    count = Counter(nums)
    sorted_items = sorted(count.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
    return [item[0] for item in sorted_items[:k]]
problems.constraint_violating_solution:
def top_k_frequent(nums, k):
    from collections import Counter
    count = Counter(nums)
    return list(count.values())[:k]

problems.title: Product of Array Except Self
problems.description: Given an integer array nums, return an array answer such that answer[i] is equal to the product of all the elements of nums except nums[i]. Must run in O(n) without division.
learning_objectives: Avoid invalid operations like division by zero
problems.reference_solution:
def product_except_self(nums):
    length = len(nums)
    ans = [0]*length
    ans[0] = 1
    for i in range(1, length):
        ans[i] = nums[i - 1] * ans[i - 1]
    R = 1
    for i in reversed(range(length)):
        ans[i] = ans[i] * R
        R *= nums[i]
    return ans

problems.title: Subarray Sum Equals K
problems.description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer k, return the total number of continuous subarrays whose sum equals to k.
learning_objectives: Utilize Hash Maps for difference matching

problems.title: Number of Islands
problems.description: Given an m x n 2D binary grid grid which represents a map of '1's (land) and '0's (water), return the number of islands.
learning_objectives: Prevent cycles by tracking visited nodes

problems.title: Merge Intervals
problems.description: Given an array of intervals where intervals[i] = [starti, endi], merge all overlapping intervals.

problems.title: Minimum Coin Change
problems.description: You are given an integer array coins representing coins of different denominations and an integer amount. Return the fewest number of coins that you need to make up that amount.
learning_objectives: Understand when Greedy fails vs DP

problems.title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
problems.description: Given an integer array nums, return the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence.
learning_objectives: Model subset relationships through DP arrays

problems.title: Edit Distance
problems.description: Given two strings word1 and word2, return the minimum number of operations required to convert word1 to word2.
learning_objectives: Map operations to DP state transitions
problems.constraint_violating_solution:
def min_distance(word1, word2):
    diff = abs(len(word1) - len(word2))
    chars1, chars2 = set(word1), set(word2)
    diff += len(chars2 - chars1)
    return diff

problems.title: Valid Anagram
problems.description: Given two strings s and t, return true if t is an anagram of s, and false otherwise.
problems.inefficient_solution:
def is_anagram(s, t):
    return sorted(s) == sorted(t)
problems.constraint_violating_solution:
def is_anagram(s, t):
    return set(s) == set(t)

problems.title: Find the Duplicate Number
problems.description: Given an array of integers nums containing n + 1 integers where each integer is in the range [1, n] inclusive. There is only one repeated number, return it without modifying the array using constant space.
